gap_analysis: Every seat treats the 200d MA / 341-360 zone as a support shelf to buy into, but none of them address that this is a blowoff-top distribution -27.5% off high, inside an EXTENDING sector selloff, with a sentiment seat that explicitly found NO capitulation signature (short interest 1.32% flat, rec_mean unchanged, RSI not at exhaustion). A 200d test with unflushed positioning and active insider distribution is not the same setup as a 200d test after a flush — there is no capitulation low behind this level, so 'buy the 200d' is pattern-matching a support level without the volume/positioning evidence that normally makes 200d tests hold. The second, compounding gap: the Fundamental and Narrative seats both lean on the FY27 >$100B 'contracted' AI revenue figure as if it were locked backlog, but the word used publicly is 'guidance/visibility,' not signed contractual backlog with penalties — and the MediaTek/TPUv9 win at Google is live proof that even a top hyperscaler relationship can defect before that revenue is realized. The moat story (3-5yr design-cycle lock-in) and the valuation story (PEG 0.42 on FY27 EPS) are both leaning on the same unverified number.
tail_risk: 5-10% probability over the next 2-4 quarters: a second hyperscaler (Meta or Amazon, both dual-sourcing custom silicon) follows Google's MediaTek move and diversifies a slice of its next-gen ASIC roadmap away from Broadcom, or Q3/Q4 AI-semi guidance is cut (not just 'reiterated') below the $100B FY27 run-rate implied by consensus. Given the stock already dropped -12% on a mere no-raise this quarter, a real guide-down on top of a sector-wide SOXX unwind plausibly takes AVGO through the weekly EMA52 (341) and toward the next real support near the pre-AI-cycle base, roughly $260-290 — a further -20% to -25% from current 358, i.e., -45% to -47% from the 495 high. [LIVE-derived estimate, not a published target]
historical_analog: NVIDIA, Nov 2021 to Oct 2022: NVDA peaked near a split-adjusted $33.38 in Nov 2021 amid a euphoric AI/gaming/crypto-demand narrative, heavy insider selling under 10b5-1 plans, and near-unanimous analyst bullishness, then fell 66% to a trough of $11.23 by Oct 2022 — despite near-term earnings still being reported as 'fine' for several of the intervening quarters before the crypto-demand air pocket and datacenter digestion showed up in the numbers. The pattern match to AVGO: a dominant, high-quality franchise at a blowoff top, insiders selling into strength, consensus untouched through the first leg down, and the eventual drawdown driven by a growth/guidance disappointment rather than any fundamental broken-business thesis. [MEM, price levels verified via web search 2026-07-02]
invalidation_a: Q3 FY26 AI-semi revenue guide is RAISED (not merely reiterated) above the $17.2B street number that was just missed, with management explicitly quantifying signed/contracted (not just 'visible') backlog for FY27 — this would convert the $100B figure from a forecast into a fact and remove the central valuation-support gap.
invalidation_b: No second hyperscaler follows Google/MediaTek within the next 2 reported quarters, AND at least one of Meta/Amazon/ByteDance publicly reaffirms or expands its multi-year custom-silicon commitment with Broadcom specifically — this would falsify the 'design-cycle lock-in is cracking' thesis.
invalidation_c: Insider transactions flip: cluster of open-market BUYS (not 10b5-1 scheduled sells) from Samueli, Spears, Brazeal, or other Section 16 officers within the current 341-360 zone, at size comparable to the June sell cluster — insiders voting with capital that this level is cheap would directly contradict the DISTRIBUTING(HIGH) smart-money read.
portfolio_tail_stress: At the proposed ~$3,500 total position (0.35% of the ~$1M tradfi book): a -30% draw costs -$1,050, a -50% draw costs -$1,750 — both immaterial to the book in isolation. The real risk is not this position's dollar size, it's the pattern: this is a recurring type of trade (scale into every AI-semi/AI-infra name on a dip toward its 200d) being executed while the desk's own macro stance (per Lyn Alden / Dalio / Hunt lenses already in memory) is late-cycle/AI-bubble-defense. Adding $2,100 here is the same behavioral bet as the last several adds — if the AI-capex cycle turns, ALL of those positions draw down together, correlated, on the same day, and the aggregate tail loss across the AI-name basket is what matters, not the AVGO stub in isolation. One name at 0.35% is a rounding error; five or six AI names each at 0.3-0.5% bought on the same 'buy every 200d test' playbook is a real correlated bet the size-limits per position are designed to obscure.

controlling_factor: The 200d MA is being bought as if it were a flushed, capitulated low, but sentiment shows zero capitulation and smart-money shows active insider distribution into this exact zone — that combination, not the valuation math, is why this is not a level to add on.
